title: When He Should Laugh by Edward Thomas — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
WHEN he should laugh the wise man knows full well:
For he knows what is truly laughable.
But wiser is the man who laughs also,
Or holds his laughter, when the foolish do.
meaning: "When He Should Laugh" by Edward Thomas is a deep english poem consisting of 4 lines. This English poem by Edward Thomas demonstrates the timeless power of verse to capture complex human emotions. Beginning with "WHEN he should laugh the wise man knows full well: For he knows what is truly laughable....", this piece explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
